title: Baby Sleep Hours by Age 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Much Sleep Does Your Baby Need by Age?
description: Discover how many hours your baby needs to sleep at each age, plus recommended naps and wake windows. Get personalized sleep guidance based on NSF & AAP…
seoKeywords: how much sleep does a baby need
how long should a baby sleep
intro: Sleep is essential for your baby's development. Sleep needs vary greatly by age: a **newborn sleeps 16-17 hours** and a 3-year-old around **12 hours**. This calculator shows **recommended sleep hours**, the number of **naps**, and **wake windows** by age, based on **NSF** and **AAP** guidelines.
keyTakeaway: A newborn sleeps **16-17 hours**, a 6-month-old **14-15 hours**, and a 2-year-old **12-14 hours**. Wake windows range from **45-60 minutes** (newborn) to **5-6 hours** (2-3 years).
useCases: You want to know if your baby is getting enough sleep.
You need to set up a nap schedule.
Your baby wakes frequently and you want to understand wake windows.
You're wondering how many naps your baby should take.
Your pediatrician asked about your baby's sleep hours.

example.title: Example: 6-month-old baby
example.result: Your **6-month-old** needs **14-15 hours** of total sleep per day: **10-11 hours** at night + **2-3 naps** of 30-90 minutes each. Wake window: **2-3 hours**.

explanation:
## Sleep by Age Chart

| Age | Total Hours | Nighttime Sleep | Naps | Wake Window |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0-1 month | 16-17 h | 8-9 h | 4-5 (irregular) | 45-60 min |
| 1-3 months | 15-16 h | 9-10 h | 3-4 | 1-1.5 h |
| 3-6 months | 14-15 h | 10-11 h | 2-3 | 1.5-2.5 h |
| 6-9 months | 14-15 h | 10-11 h | 2-3 | 2-3 h |
| 9-12 months | 13-14 h | 10-12 h | 2 | 2.5-3.5 h |
| 12-18 months | 13-14 h | 11-12 h | 1-2 | 3-4.5 h |
| 18-24 months | 12-14 h | 11-12 h | 1 | 4.5-5.5 h |
| 2-3 years | 12-13 h | 10-12 h | 0-1 | 5-6 h |
| 3-5 years | 11-13 h | 10-12 h | 0-1 | 6+ h |

## Signs Your Baby Is Tired

- Rubbing eyes
- Yawning
- Glazed or vacant expression
- Irritability
- Pulling at ears

## General Tips for Better Sleep

- **Consistent routine**: bath, pajamas, story, lullaby → crib.
- **Dark and cool environment** (18-20°C).
- **White noise**: helps many babies fall asleep.
- **Put baby down drowsy but awake** (from 4 months onward).

## Related Calculators

- [Baby Growth Percentile](/baby-growth-percentile) — growth tracking.
- [Baby Development Milestones](/baby-development-milestones) — developmental progress.
- [Baby Vaccination Schedule](/baby-vaccination-schedule) — immunization calendar.

faq.q: How much sleep does a 6-month-old baby need?
faq.a: A 6-month-old needs **14-15 hours** of total sleep per day: approximately **10-11 hours** at night and **2-3 naps** during the day (30-90 minutes each).

faq.q: What is a wake window, and why does it matter?
faq.a: A wake window is the maximum amount of time your baby can stay awake without becoming overtired. If you exceed it, your baby becomes overstimulated and harder to put down. Respecting wake windows is key to healthy sleep habits.

faq.q: When do babies transition from 3 naps to 2 naps?
faq.a: The transition from 3 to 2 naps typically happens between **6-9 months**. Signs include: the third nap becomes very short or your baby resists it, and wake windows naturally extend.

faq.q: Is it normal for my newborn to wake every 2-3 hours?
faq.a: Yes, it's **completely normal**. Newborns need to feed every 2-3 hours and have very short sleep cycles (~45 minutes). It's exhausting, but this is part of normal development and will improve gradually.

faq.q: Is it safe for my baby to sleep on their back?
faq.a: Yes, the **AAP recommends placing your baby on their back** at all times until age 12 months to prevent Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS). Use a firm crib with no pillows, blankets, or stuffed animals.

faq.q: When do toddlers stop taking naps?
faq.a: Most children stop napping between **3-5 years old**. Some quit earlier (around age 2.5) and others need naps until age 5. If your child resists naps but becomes irritable in the afternoon, they may still need that rest.

faq.q: What should I do if my baby won't sleep?
faq.a: Check these factors: appropriate wake window, dark and cool room (18-20°C), predictable routine, and that your baby isn't hungry, gassy, or has a dirty diaper. If problems persist, consult your pediatrician to rule out reflux, allergies, or other issues.

faq.q: What are signs my baby is tired?
faq.a: Common tired cues include: rubbing eyes, yawning, glazed expression, irritability, and pulling at ears. Recognizing these signs and putting your baby down quickly helps prevent overtiredness.

sources.name: NSF — How Much Sleep Do Children Need?
